# RectAp { #astroapers.RectAp }

```python
RectAp(positions, w, h, theta=0.0, *, validate=True)
```

Rotated rectangular aperture in pixel coordinates.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**positions**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple or array - like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center or centers as ``(x, y)`` pixel coordinates. Multiple centers are accepted as an ``(N, 2)`` array-like object.

<code>[**w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Full rectangle width and height in pixels. ``w`` is measured along the rectangle's local x axis, and ``h`` along its local y axis. At ``theta=0``, the width axis is aligned with the image x axis and the height axis is aligned with the image y axis.

<code>[**h**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Full rectangle width and height in pixels. ``w`` is measured along the rectangle's local x axis, and ``h`` along its local y axis. At ``theta=0``, the width axis is aligned with the image x axis and the height axis is aligned with the image y axis.

<code>[**theta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Rotation angle in radians, measured counterclockwise from the positive image x axis to the rectangle's local width axis.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, skip input validation and assume finite, positive geometry and normalized positions.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [bboxes](#astroapers.RectAp.bboxes) | Override the default python for-loop with a Rust-accelerated batch function. |

### bboxes { #astroapers.RectAp.bboxes }

```python
RectAp.bboxes()
```

Override the default python for-loop with a Rust-accelerated batch function.
